The '''flame battle droid''' was a [[Flamethrower/Legends|flamethrower]]-equipped variant of the [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-series battle droid]] produced by the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] that was used during the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]] and the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].

==Characteristics==
Instead of using [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]], flame [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] used [[Flamethrower/Legends|flame jets]] that could not be blocked by a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi's]] [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]. A large backpack was attached to the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] that contained the necessary flammable gases. They had [[Color/Legends|tan]] plating,<ref name="SWJPB" /> and were distinguished from their [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-series battle droid]] cousins by the red coloration of the droid's shoulder plates.<ref name="CSWE" /> They were [[Programmer/Legends|programmed]] to terrorize innocent civilians and burn down homes, crops, and municipal buildings to destroy morale.<ref name="JPB guide" /> Another variant of the flame battle droid existed; the other variant featured blue markings and carried a different type of flamethrower.<ref name="Star Wars: Obi-Wan video game">[[Star Wars: Obi-Wan (video game)|''Star Wars: Obi-Wan'']]</ref>

==History==
Flame battle droids were produced<ref name="CSWE" /> and used by the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] during the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]]. A group was guarding the entrance to the [[Theed Royal Palace/Legends|Theed Royal Palace]] along with a [[T4 turret droid]]. They were [[Capture of Theed/Legends|destroyed]] by the Jedi<ref name="SWJPB" /> [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] and [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]].<ref>[[Star Wars: Episode I Jedi Power Battles|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Jedi Power Battles'']] can be played with one or two characters; these characters are [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]], [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], [[Plo Koon/Legends|Plo Koon]], [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]], and [[Adi Gallia/Legends|Adi Gallia]]. Any character can be selected from the loading screen for any of the game's 10 missions. This article assumes the selection of Jinn and Kenobi, per their appearance in {{Film|I}}.</ref> Other flame droids [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|guarded]] a group of [[Royal Naboo Security Forces/Legends|Royal Naboo Security Forces]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]].<ref name="SWJPB" /> Flame battle droids continued to see use by the Trade Federation<ref name="Weapons and Technology">''[[The New Essential Guide to Weapons and Technology]]''</ref> and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].<ref name="CSWE" />

==Behind the scenes==
Flame battle droids first appeared in the [[2000]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Episode I Jedi Power Battles]]''.<ref name="SWJPB" />

==Appearances==
*[[Star Wars: Obi-Wan (video game)|''Star Wars: Obi-Wan'']]
